transformation_type: ring-opening / ring-closing heterocycle isomerization with heteroatom translocation
mechanism_type: ANRORC-type nucleophilic opening followed by bond rotation and ring closure / heteroatom migration
common_conditions: treat nitrogen-rich heterocycle with nucleophile or base, often under heating, to induce ring opening and reclosure to an isomeric heterocycle
description_short: Ring-opening / ring-closing rearrangement of heterocycles that translocates heteroatoms and attached substituents to deliver isomeric aza-heterocycles or rearranged nucleosides.

transformation_type: one-carbon homologation of alkenes to allenes via dihalocyclopropane intermediates
mechanism_type: dihalocarbene cyclopropanation followed by metal–halogen exchange and cyclopropylidene-to-allene rearrangement
common_conditions: generate dihalocarbene from haloform under basic conditions, add across alkene, then treat the geminal dihalocyclopropane with organolithium or metal to rearrange to an allene
description_short: Classical allene synthesis in which dihalocarbene addition to an alkene gives a geminal dihalocyclopropane that is rearranged under metal-mediated conditions to an allene.

transformation_type: benzannulation of Fischer chromium carbene complexes with alkynes to hydroquinone / phenol derivatives
mechanism_type: alkyne insertion into chromium carbene followed by CO loss and benzannulation
common_conditions: prepare Fischer-type chromium carbene, combine with alkyne under thermal or photochemical conditions, then unveil hydroquinone / aromatic products after benzannulation
description_short: Chromium-carbene annulation with alkynes that forms aromatic rings, often hydroquinone derivatives, through a Dötz benzannulation sequence involving CO extrusion.

transformation_type: auxiliary-controlled asymmetric alpha-alkylation of carbonyl compounds via chiral hydrazones
mechanism_type: azaenolate formation and facially selective electrophilic substitution directed by SAMP / RAMP auxiliaries
common_conditions: form chiral hydrazone from carbonyl compound and SAMP or RAMP auxiliary, deprotonate with strong base, alkylate with electrophile, then unmask or transform the hydrazone product
description_short: Asymmetric alpha-functionalization strategy that uses chiral SAMP or RAMP hydrazones to direct diastereoselective alkylation of aldehydes and ketones.

transformation_type: catalytic redistribution of alkene and alkyne multiple bonds to conjugated 1,3-dienes
mechanism_type: metal-carbene metathesis via metallacyclobutene intermediates in ring-closing or cross-enyne modes
key_reagents_clue: enyne metathesis|Grubbs catalyst|ruthenium carbene|ring-closing enyne metathesis|1,3-diene
common_conditions: expose 1,n-enyne or alkene/alkyne pair to ruthenium, platinum, or related metathesis catalyst to form cyclic or acyclic 1,3-dienes
description_short: Metathesis reaction between an alkene and an alkyne that generates conjugated dienes, commonly under Grubbs-type ruthenium catalysis in ring-closing or cross-metathesis formats.
